title: The Ultimate Guide to 10 Herbal Teas for Dry Lips
intro_desc: Herbal teas for dry lips are infusions made from plants that help to soothe, moisturize, and protect dry, chapped lips. They work by providing essential fatty acids, antioxidants, and anti-inflammatory properties to combat dryness and irritation. The best herbal teas for dry lips include Calendula officinalis, Aloe vera, Symphytum officinale, Chamomilla recutita, Echinacea purpurea, Melissa officinalis, Rosmarinus officinalis, Hibiscus sabdariffa, Aloe barbadensis, and Silybum marianum. Without proper treatment, dry lips can lead to discomfort, difficulty eating, speaking, and even affect self-confidence and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tea is a natural remedy that can help soothe dry lips. The tea is made from the gel of the aloe vera plant, which is rich in vitamins and minerals. Aloe vera contains vitamin E, which helps to lock in moisture and protect dry skin from further damage. When you drink aloe vera tea, the antioxidants and vitamins are absorbed into your body, helping to hydrate your skin from the inside out. This can be especially helpful for dry lips, as it can help to replenish moisture and reduce inflammation. The antibacterial properties of aloe vera can also help to prevent infections and soothe irritations on dry lips. Additionally, aloe vera tea can help to calm and cool dry, chapped lips, making it a great natural remedy for dry lip care.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for dry lips because they have soothing properties that help to reduce inflammation, calm irritation, and lock in moisture to keep lips hydrated and smooth.
Flavonoids in LAVANDULA ANGUSTIFOLIA are good for dry lips because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect dry, chapped skin.
Polysaccharides help dry lips by forming a protective barrier on the skin, locking in moisture and soothing irritation to leave lips feeling smoother and more hydrated.
Vitamins are good for dry lips because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and moisturize dry, chapped skin, reducing irritation and promoting healthy lip texture.
Glucomannans are good for dry lips because they help lock in moisture and protect the skin from losing water, reducing dryness and cracking.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal teas for dry lips
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for dry lips, you will need a handful of fresh or dried lavender flowers, a teapot, a cup, and a strainer. First, add one tablespoon of the flowers to a teapot, then pour in a cup of boiling water. Allow the mixture to steep for about 5-7 minutes, until the liquid turns a light purple color. Next, strain the tea into a cup using a strainer or cheesecloth to remove the lavender flowers. You can then add honey or sugar to taste, if you prefer a sweeter flavor. Let the tea cool down before applying it to your dry lips. To do this, soak a cotton ball or a clean cloth in the cooled tea and gently rub it onto your lips. The antiseptic and moisturizing properties of the Lavandula angustifolia will help to soothe and heal your dry lips, leaving them feeling smooth and refreshed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tea is a great remedy for dry, chapped lips. This is because calendula is rich in antioxidants and fatty acids that help to lock in moisture and soothe dry skin. The tea also contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling, making it an effective treatment for cracked and painful lips. When applied topically, the tea's active ingredients can help to repair and regenerate the skin, promoting the growth of new, healthy cells. Additionally, calendula tea has antimicrobial properties that prevent the spread of infections, which can further irritate dry lips. Regular use of calendula tea can help to keep lips soft, smooth, and hydrated, even in dry and cold weather.

remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal teas for dry lips
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with dry lips, you'll need some fresh or dried lavender flowers. Start by boiling a cup of water, then remove it from heat. Add one to two teaspoons of fresh lavender flowers or one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers to the water. Let it steep for five to seven minutes, allowing the lavender's healing properties to infuse into the water. After the steeping time, strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to catch the lavender flowers. You can drink this tea as it is, or you can add a bit of honey to sweeten it. The lavender tea's soothing and anti-inflammatory properties will help to calm and moisturize your dry lips, while its antiseptic properties will prevent infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that can help soothe dry lips. The tea is made from the flowers of the Echinacea plant, which is rich in antioxidants and other beneficial compounds. When applied topically, the tea can provide relief from dry, chapped lips. The antioxidants in the tea help to lock in moisture and protect the skin from damage caused by the environment and dry air. This can help to reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles, leaving lips feeling softer and more supple. The anti-inflammatory properties of the tea can also help to reduce redness and irritation, providing a calming effect on the skin. By drinking Echinacea purpurea tea, you may be able to help boost your body's natural defenses against dryness and promote overall health from the inside out.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with dry lips, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Lavender flowers, a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup, and boiling water. Begin by measuring out one tablespoon of dried Lavender flowers and placing it in your tea infuser or the heat-resistant cup. Next, boil fresh water and pour it over the Lavender flowers, making sure the flowers are completely submerged. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the Lavender's soothing properties to infuse into the water. After the steeping time has passed, carefully lift the infuser or remove the Lavender flowers from the cup, and discard the flowers. Your Lavandula angustifolia tea is now ready to use as a natural remedy for dry lips. To apply, simply dip a cotton swab into the tea, and gently apply it to your dry lips, allowing the soothing properties of the Lavender to calm and moisturize your skin.

When applied topically, the tea can provide a cooling sensation that helps to lock in moisture and protect the lips from further dryness. Additionally, Melissa officinalis tea has antimicrobial properties that can help to combat infections and promote healthy skin, which can contribute to the healing of dry and cracked lips. Drinking the tea internally may also help to hydrate the body from the inside out, promoting overall moisture levels and potentially alleviating dry lip symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tea, also known as peppermint tea, is a great remedy for dry lips. remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for dry lips
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Lavandula angustifolia herbal tea that helps with dry lips, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried lavender flowers. If using fresh flowers, rinse them gently with cold water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, combine the lavender flowers with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the liquid has cooled to a comfortable temperature. Strain the tea into a separate container, discarding the lavender flowers. You can then use this tea as a mouthwash by swishing it around your mouth for 30 seconds to 1 minute before spitting it out. Alternatively, you can apply the cooled tea to your dry lips using a clean cotton ball or a soft cloth, allowing the tea to dry before reapplying it as needed.

supplementary_best: To remedy dry lips, a combination of Aloe vera, Calendula officinalis, and Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas is the most effective. Aloe vera tea provides intense moisturizing properties, helping to hydrate and soothe dry, chapped lips. Calendula officinalis tea has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce redness and prevent infections that can make dry lips worse. Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root, has soothing and protective properties that help to calm and shield the skin from further dryness and irritation. This combination of teas works together to provide long-lasting hydration, reduce inflammation, and protect the lips from further damage.
supplementary_avoid: If you have dry lips, there are some herbal teas you should avoid. Peppermint tea can be too drying due to its high menthol content, which can strip your skin of its natural oils and make dry lips worse. Another tea to avoid is chamomile tea, which can cause your skin to dry out further because it has a mild diuretic effect that can reduce moisture levels in the body. Sage tea is also not recommended, as it can dry out your skin and increase saliva production, which can dry out your lips even more. Also, avoid drinking tea made from licorice root, as it can cause dehydration and dryness in the skin. Similarly, drinking tea made from yerba mate can be drying due to its high caffeine content, which can increase urine production and lead to dehydration. Lastly, avoid tea made from chamomile's relative, German chamomile, as it also has a mild diuretic effect that can dry out your skin and lips.
supplementary_other_remedies: Using aloe vera gel on dry lips can help soothe and moisturize them. Aloe vera has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and irritation. It's also rich in vitamins and minerals that can help lock in moisture and promote healthy skin.  Coconut oil can also be used to help dry lips. It's a natural emollient that can help fill gaps between skin cells, making lips feel softer and smoother. Applying coconut oil to dry lips can also help protect them from the elements and keep them hydrated for longer.  Honey can be used to help dry lips because it's a natural humectant that can help retain moisture in the skin. When applied to dry lips, honey can help attract and hold onto water, keeping lips hydrated and comfortable.  Olive oil can also be used to help dry lips. It's rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage and promote healthy cell growth. Applying olive oil to dry lips can help nourish and moisturize them, leaving them feeling softer and more comfortable.  Ghee, or clarified butter, can be used to help dry lips because it's rich in fatty acids that can help moisturize and nourish the skin. When applied to dry lips, ghee can help lock in moisture and protect them from the elements.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with dry lips often experience other skin problems, like eczema and psoriasis. These conditions are caused by inflammation and irritation of the skin. Aloe vera is a medicinal herb that can help heal dry, itchy skin. It has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and soothe irritated skin. Aloe vera gel can be applied directly to the affected areas to provide relief.  Dry lips can also be a symptom of vitamin deficiencies, such as a lack of vitamin B12. This vitamin is essential for healthy skin and mucous membranes. Plantain is a medicinal herb that is rich in vitamin B12. It can be made into a tea or taken as a supplement to help increase vitamin levels in the body.  Dry lips can also be a sign of dehydration. When the body doesn't have enough water, the skin can become dry and cracked. Herbal teas like peppermint and chamomile can help rehydrate the body and soothe dry skin. These teas can also calm irritation and reduce inflammation.  People with dry lips may also experience mouth sores and canker sores. These sores are caused by irritation and inflammation of the mucous membranes in the mouth. Licorice root is a medicinal herb that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help heal mouth sores. It can be made into a tea or taken as a supplement to provide relief.
